_description: BOUNDARY TEST: Proficient max (7.5) vs Expert min (7.6). Tests Expert gating: requires ≥1 S5 AND ≥1 S6, both at clear+.

case_A_proficient_max._description: Strong Proficient evidence but no S5/S6 — must stay at Proficient (≤7.5).

signals.excerpt: Training depth must match role risk: I scale from a 30-min orientation for low-touch roles to 12 hours for direct client contact roles because the failure modes are completely different.
Adult learning works through problem-centered practice, not lecture — so our training uses case simulations.
For tech-averse volunteers I redesign training to use paper job aids; for expert volunteers I skip basics and focus on org-specific nuance.
Crisis-line volunteers get trauma-informed support debriefs that we don't run for event volunteers.
I proactively identify volunteers who seem disengaged and schedule check-ins before they quit.

dimensions._rationale: No S5 or S6 signals at all. Expert requires both. Proficient is the highest qualifying tier.

case_B_expert_min._description: Meets Expert floor with exactly 1 S5 (clear) + 1 S6 (clear) + ≥2 Expert signals total.

signals.excerpt: I designed a three-level development pathway: frontline → team lead → trainer, with competency gates at each transition that I review annually against role performance data.
I trained two staff members to become volunteer trainers themselves — now they run onboarding without me.
Adult learning principles guide my approach.

dimensions._rationale: ≥2 Expert-tier signals (S5+S6 at clear). ≥1 S5 ✓ at clear+. ≥1 S6 ✓ at clear+. Expert qualifies.
dimensions._score_calc: v1.1: Σ at Expert+: 1.0+1.0 = 2.0; Q=2.0, K=6.0 → position = max(0, (2.0−2.0)/4.0) = 0; score = 7.6 (band floor per R2)
